title: Reflecting on a year of Claude Code
summary: Boris Cherny and Cat Wu reflect on Claude Code's first year as a generally available agentic coding tool running in terminals worldwide. Topics include verification best practices, auto mode design, adoption beyond engineering, and emerging patterns like context minimalism and agent orchestration. The discussion covers how engineering teams have integrated Claude Code across entire codebases and how agent-based workflows are reshaping developer responsibilities.
writeup: Anthropic leaders mark the first year of general availability for Claude Code, an agentic coding tool, and review the design choices behind it. The discussion covers verification approaches, auto mode design, and adoption patterns observed over the year. Architectural decisions get specific attention: context minimalism, agent orchestration, and code review automation.
